zotero.abstractNote: Generative models are increasingly used in the sciences to model complex data distributions. Evaluating these models requires comparing the distribution of generated samples to the distribution of ground-truth data. While many statistical distances exist, choosing the right one for a specific scientific application is often difficult. In this paper, we provide a practical guide to sample-based statistical distances for evaluating generative models. We review common distances, discuss their properties, and provide recommendations for their use in scientific applications. We also provide a software library that implements these distances and facilitates their use in practice.
